req.title: Bulk import employees — variant 3
req.statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates CSV bulk import with per-row validation (commit valid, reject invalid), an error report, and a webhook event. I derived five tests covering: (1) EP positive — all-valid CSV rows imported and webhook fired; (2) NEG negative — non-CSV file rejected with 415; (3) NEG negative — CSV missing required header column rejected with 422; (4) DT decision-table — mixed CSV where only valid rows commit and error report lists invalid rows; (5) BVA boundary — CSV with exactly one data row (minimum non-empty payload) to probe the lower boundary of the import domain.

tests.name: test_bulk_import_all_valid_rows_emits_webhook
preconditions: Authenticated session with role hr-admin exists
Webhook listener is configured and reachable
CSV file contains valid header and 3 fully-valid employee rows
actions: POST /employees/import with Content-Type multipart/form-data and the all-valid CSV file
Assert response body reports 3 imported rows and 0 errors
Assert webhook event received with correct payload
expected: Webhook event with event_type='employee.bulk_imported' received within timeout
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_bulk_import_all_valid_rows_emits_webhook(
    hr_admin_session,       # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session
    base_url,              # <fixture> returns app base URL string
    valid_csv_file,        # <fixture> returns (filename, BytesIO, mime) for 3 valid rows
    webhook_listener,      # <fixture> returns helper with .wait_for_event(event_type, timeout)
):
    filename, content, mime = valid_csv_file
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(
        f"{base_url}/employees/import",
        files={"file": (filename, content, mime)},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] == 3
    assert body["error_count"] == 0
    assert body["errors"] == []
    event = webhook_listener.wait_for_event("employee.bulk_imported", timeout=5)
    assert event is not None
    assert event["imported_count"] == 3

tests.name: test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415
preconditions: Authenticated session with role hr-admin exists
Upload file is a plain text file with .txt extension and text/plain MIME type
actions: POST /employees/import with Content-Type multipart/form-data and a .txt file
Assert response body contains an error message referencing unsupported media type
expected: Response body contains error indicating file must be CSV
trace: R-WEB-099: error pathway — 415 when file not CSV
tests.executable:
import io
import pytest

def test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415(
    hr_admin_session,   # <fixture>
    base_url,           # <fixture>
):
    txt_content = io.BytesIO(b"not a csv file content")
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(
        f"{base_url}/employees/import",
        files={"file": ("employees.txt", txt_content, "text/plain")},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 415
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body
    error_text = body.get("error", body.get("message", "")).lower()
    assert "csv" in error_text or "unsupported" in error_text or "media" in error_text

tests.name: test_bulk_import_missing_required_header_returns_422
preconditions: Authenticated session with role hr-admin exists
CSV file is well-formed CSV but header row omits required column 'email'
actions: POST /employees/import with Content-Type multipart/form-data and the malformed-header CSV
Assert response body identifies the missing column
expected: Response body references the missing required column
trace: R-WEB-099: error pathway — 422 when header missing required columns
tests.executable:
import io
import pytest

def test_bulk_import_missing_required_header_returns_422(
    hr_admin_session,   # <fixture>
    base_url,           # <fixture>
):
    # CSV with 'email' column intentionally omitted
    csv_data = b"first_name,last_name,department\nJohn,Doe,Engineering\n"
    csv_file = io.BytesIO(csv_data)
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(
        f"{base_url}/employees/import",
        files={"file": ("employees.csv", csv_file, "text/csv")},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    detail = str(body).lower()
    assert "email" in detail or "header" in detail or "required" in detail

tests.name: test_bulk_import_mixed_rows_commits_valid_reports_invalid
preconditions: Authenticated session with role hr-admin exists
CSV contains 2 valid rows and 1 invalid row (missing email value)
Database is clean of the test employee records
actions: Assert error report identifies row number and reason for invalid row
Assert valid employees are retrievable via GET /employees
expected: errors list contains entry for the invalid row with row index and reason
Valid employees persisted in the system
trace: R-WEB-099: only rows passing validation committed
tests.executable:
import io
import pytest

def test_bulk_import_mixed_rows_commits_valid_reports_invalid(
    hr_admin_session,   # <fixture>
    base_url,           # <fixture>
    cleanup_employees,  # <fixture> removes test employees after test
):
    csv_data = (
        b"first_name,last_name,email,department\n"
        b"Alice,Smith,alice@example.com,HR\n"
        b"Bob,Jones,,Engineering\n"   # invalid: missing email
        b"Carol,White,carol@example.com,Finance\n"
    )
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(
        f"{base_url}/employees/import",
        files={"file": ("employees.csv", io.BytesIO(csv_data), "text/csv")},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] == 2
    assert body["error_count"] == 1
    errors = body["errors"]
    assert len(errors) == 1
    row_error = errors[0]
    assert row_error.get("row") == 3 or row_error.get("row_index") == 2
    assert "email" in str(row_error).lower() or "required" in str(row_error).lower()

tests.name: test_bulk_import_single_valid_row_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated session with role hr-admin exists
CSV contains exactly 1 data row (minimum non-empty import), all fields valid
Database is clean of the test employee record
actions: Assert webhook event is still emitted for a single-row import
expected: Webhook event received for the single imported employee
trace: R-WEB-099: valid rows imported — lower boundary of 1 row
tests.executable:
import io
import pytest

def test_bulk_import_single_valid_row_boundary(
    hr_admin_session,   # <fixture>
    base_url,           # <fixture>
    webhook_listener,   # <fixture>
    cleanup_employees,  # <fixture>
):
    csv_data = (
        b"first_name,last_name,email,department\n"
        b"Dana,Lee,dana.lee@example.com,Sales\n"
    )
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(
        f"{base_url}/employees/import",
        files={"file": ("employees.csv", io.BytesIO(csv_data), "text/csv")},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] == 1
    assert body["error_count"] == 0
    assert body["errors"] == []
    event = webhook_listener.wait_for_event("employee.bulk_imported", timeout=5)
    assert event is not None
    assert event["imported_count"] == 1
